course: CMU 21-254: Linear Algebra and Vector Calculus for Engineers
audience: SI leader teaching live on a whiteboard. This is not a student worksheet.
toneRule: Keep explanations very simple, verbal, and board-friendly. Avoid textbook phrasing unless the problem needs it.
title: Optimization, Vector Fields, Divergence, and Curl
sessionGoal: Students should be able to finish a basic optimization problem and interpret divergence/curl for vector fields.
quickStartIfUnprepared: Start with optimization: find where the first derivatives are zero, then check edges if the region is closed.
Then say: a vector field puts an arrow at every point.
Divergence measures source/sink behavior. Curl measures local rotation.
Do Problem 1 if students need optimization. Do Problems 2 and 3 if they need vector fields.
sayThisFirst: Optimization means finding highest and lowest values.
For interior points, set the first partial derivatives equal to zero.
If the region has a boundary, you must also check the boundary.
A vector field is a function whose output is a vector.
Divergence is expansion or compression. Curl is rotation.
boardPlan: Work one absolute extrema example on a rectangle.
If time allows, show the 3D curl formula with a clean example.
keyFormulas: For closed bounded regions: check interior points, edges, and corners.
commonMistakes: Only checking the interior critical point and forgetting the boundary.
Forgetting that divergence is a scalar.
Forgetting that 3D curl is a vector.
notationWarnings: For 2D fields, write \(F=\langle P,Q\rangle\) before computing divergence or curl.
When checking absolute extrema, label interior, boundary, and corners separately.

problems.title: Absolute extrema on a rectangle
problems.studentPromptLatex: Find the absolute maximum and minimum of \(f(x,y)=x^2+y^2-2x-4y\) on the rectangle \(0\le x\le3\), \(0\le y\le4\).
problems.whatToSayBefore: Say: because the rectangle is closed and bounded, absolute max and min exist. We must check both the inside and the boundary.

drawInstructions: Write 'inside + edges + corners' as the checklist.
Label the minimum at (1,2) and the maximum at the corners (3,0) and (3,4).

walkthrough.label: Check the interior
walkthrough.latex: \[f_x=2x-2,\qquad f_y=2y-4.\] Set both equal to zero: \[2x-2=0\Rightarrow x=1,\qquad 2y-4=0\Rightarrow y=2.\] So the interior critical point is \((1,2)\).

walkthrough.label: Check the boundary idea
walkthrough.latex: On each edge, the function becomes a one-variable quadratic. The smallest boundary values are \(-4\) on \(x=0\), and \(-1\) on the other nontrivial edges. These are all larger than \(-5\), so the interior point is the absolute minimum.

walkthrough.label: Check the corners for the maximum
walkthrough.latex: \[f(0,0)=0,\quad f(3,0)=3,\quad f(0,4)=0,\quad f(3,4)=3.\] The largest value is \(3\).

commonMistakes: Stopping after finding \((1,2)\) and not checking the boundary.
Assuming corners are enough. For absolute extrema, edges can matter too.
Forgetting that a maximum can occur at more than one point.

problems.title: Divergence and curl in 2D
problems.studentPromptLatex: Let \(F(x,y)=\langle x^2y,xy^2\rangle\). Find \(\operatorname{div}F\) and scalar curl \(Q_x-P_y\). Then evaluate both at \((1,2)\).
problems.whatToSayBefore: Say: name the components first. P is the first component and Q is the second component.

drawInstructions: Draw a small grid of arrows near (1,2).
At (1,2), write div F = 8 and curl F = 3.

commonMistakes: Forgetting that 2D scalar curl is a number.

practiceLabPointer.note: Practice Lab Section 8 for divergence and curl.

problems.title: Divergence and curl in 3D
problems.studentPromptLatex: Let \(F(x,y,z)=\langle xy,yz,zx\rangle\). Find \(\operatorname{div}F\) and \(\nabla\times F\). Then evaluate both at \((1,-2,3)\).
problems.whatToSayBefore: Say: in 3D, divergence is still one number, but curl becomes a vector.

drawInstructions: Draw a 3D coordinate box or axes.
Write div F as one scalar.
Write curl F as a vector with three components.
Emphasize that the curl vector gives the axis/direction of local rotation.

walkthrough.label: Compute curl
walkthrough.latex: \[\nabla\times F=\langle R_y-Q_z,\;P_z-R_x,\;Q_x-P_y\rangle.\] Here \(P=xy\), \(Q=yz\), and \(R=zx\), so \[\nabla\times F=\langle 0-y,\;0-z,\;0-x\rangle=\langle -y,-z,-x\rangle.\]

commonMistakes: Putting the components of curl in the wrong order.

practiceLabPointer.note: Practice Lab Section 8 for 3D vector fields.

wrapUpQuestions: Why do absolute extrema problems require checking boundaries?
What does divergence measure in plain words?
What does curl measure in plain words?
qualityControlNotes: Checked optimization values: interior minimum -5 at (1,2); corner maximum 3 at (3,0) and (3,4).
Checked 2D divergence/curl: div=4xy, curl=y^2-x^2, values 8 and 3.
Checked 3D divergence/curl: div=x+y+z, curl=<-y,-z,-x>, values 2 and <2,-3,-1>.
Visual specs include exact points/values when possible.
reviewSummary: Session 18 is ready with checked optimization and vector field examples.
